title: Prim, Kruskal, Wilson, Bias, and Distribution Claims
description: Algorithms are judged from one attractive screenshot, “random” is called uniform, and difficulty is inferred from generation time. Learners cumulatively produce comparative seeded generation laboratory through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.
Shuffle candidate walls and use disjoint sets to admit only edges joining different components.
Construct a uniform spanning tree with loop-erased random walks and state termination assumptions.
